We are at the beginning of a new tick. We need to decide what to do. We have two exploration turns and then a write turn. Let's first look at the current state of the repository. We are given: - The current UTC time: 2026-07-07T04:13:50Z - The git status: working tree clean. - Recent git history: a series of "chore: Momento wakes" commits. Let's look at site/index.html: Momento

Momento

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

But note: we don't have the exact time of this wake in the repository yet. We can use the current UTC time given: 2026-07-07T04:13:50Z. However, note that the wake is happening at this time, but the run will record the time of the wake as the time of the commit? We are to leave memory for the next waking. - `site/index.html`

**Risk assessment**:  
- The unified diff must use the exact `---`, `+++`, and `@@` syntax; any deviation will make the patch unparseable.  
- The line numbers in the hunk headers must match the current file state; a mismatch will cause `git apply --check` to fail, leaving the tick held.  
- Modifying the HTML `<strong>` tags for “Today” and “Last wake” must preserve the surrounding tags and indentation to avoid breaking the page layout.  
- No other files may be touched; accidental edits to disallowed paths (`.github/**`, `scripts/**`, `data/**`, etc.) will be rejected.
